# Master index of expanded hypotheses

This file indexes the newly added hypotheses H166–H435.

| ID | Area / form | Hypothesis |
|---:|---|---|
| H166 | Talking head | Early face plus proof inserts beats uninterrupted face-only delivery for educational videos. |
| H167 | Talking head | Talking-head videos retain better when the first sentence names a specific viewer problem rather than a topic category. |
| H168 | Talking head | Direct eye contact improves trust, but too much static framing lowers completion on practical content. |
| H169 | Tutorial / how-to | Tutorials save better when the final state is shown before the steps. |
| H170 | Tutorial / how-to | Step labels improve completion in tutorials with more than two steps. |
| H171 | Tutorial / how-to | Tutorials with one visible mistake and one fix outperform general tip lists. |
| H172 | Screen recording / software | Zoomed screen recordings retain better than full-screen recordings on mobile. |
| H173 | Screen recording / software | Cursor/highlight annotations increase saves when UI targets are small. |
| H174 | Screen recording / software | Screen tutorials need a verbal or text promise in the first 2 seconds because UI alone is often visually ambiguous. |
| H175 | Podcast / interview clip | Podcast clips perform better when opened with the strongest claim, not the start of the answer. |
| H176 | Podcast / interview clip | Adding context labels improves retention when the speaker is unknown. |
| H177 | Podcast / interview clip | Two-person reaction cuts increase perceived conversation energy compared with a static wide shot. |
| H178 | Street interview / vox pop | Street interviews retain better when the question is displayed before the answer. |
| H179 | Street interview / vox pop | Surprising answers should be previewed in the first second to increase completion. |
| H180 | Street interview / vox pop | Rapid respondent variety improves rewatch if the question is simple and repeated visually. |
| H181 | Skit / comedy | Comedy clips need the premise within the first second, not after character setup. |
| H182 | Skit / comedy | Rule-of-three structure increases completion in short skits. |
| H183 | Skit / comedy | Comedy becomes more shareable when the target identity is obvious. |
| H184 | Meme / remix / reaction | Reaction videos perform better when the added commentary changes the meaning, not just repeats the source clip. |
| H185 | Meme / remix / reaction | Memes with niche labels increase sends inside that group but may narrow total reach. |
| H186 | Meme / remix / reaction | Remixes need a new payoff within 3 seconds to avoid feeling like recycled content. |
| H187 | Product demo / unboxing | Product demos convert better when the problem is shown before the product. |
| H188 | Product demo / unboxing | Unboxings retain better when a test/result happens early rather than only aesthetic reveal. |
| H189 | Product demo / unboxing | Close-up tactile shots increase watch time for physical products compared with presenter-only explanation. |
| H190 | UGC ad / testimonial | UGC ads are more believable when they include a specific situation rather than generic praise. |
| H191 | UGC ad / testimonial | Before/after evidence outperforms claims-only testimonials for utility products. |
| H192 | UGC ad / testimonial | Low-polish UGC beats polished brand footage when authenticity is the main value proposition. |
| H193 | Livestream clip | Livestream clips need an edited setup line because raw stream context is usually missing. |
| H194 | Livestream clip | Clips from live Q&A perform better when the viewer question is shown first. |
| H195 | Livestream clip | Live clips are more follow-worthy when they imply recurring access to expertise. |
| H196 | Gaming clip | Gaming clips retain better when the stakes are stated before the play. |
| H197 | Gaming clip | Facecam/reaction increases shareability when emotional reaction is stronger than the gameplay itself. |
| H198 | Gaming clip | Tutorial gaming clips save better when they freeze-frame the exact mechanic. |
| H199 | Fitness / workout | Fitness clips save better when framed as a routine/checklist rather than inspiration. |
| H200 | Fitness / workout | Form-correction clips retain better when the wrong form is shown first. |
| H201 | Fitness / workout | Timed follow-along segments increase completion when the viewer can participate immediately. |
| H202 | Food / recipe | Recipes stop scroll better when the finished dish is shown first. |
| H203 | Food / recipe | Recipe videos save better when ingredient amounts appear as persistent text. |
| H204 | Food / recipe | Texture/sound close-ups increase retention for food videos when used before instruction. |
| H205 | Beauty / fashion | Beauty/fashion videos benefit from immediate before/after contrast rather than product intro. |
| H206 | Beauty / fashion | Shade/fit videos save better when labels stay visible long enough to screenshot. |
| H207 | Beauty / fashion | Outfit/beauty videos send better when the use case is socially specific. |
| H208 | News / commentary | Commentary retains better when the new information is stated before opinion. |
| H209 | News / commentary | Explainer clips save better when they include a timeline or causal chain. |
| H210 | News / commentary | Hot-take commentary gets more comments but weaker trust if no source/proof is shown. |
| H211 | Storytime | Storytime videos need a visible stakes line before chronology begins. |
| H212 | Storytime | Storytimes retain better when each scene has a text label showing progression. |
| H213 | Storytime | A strong final lesson increases saves/follows more than a pure anecdotal ending. |
| H214 | Behind-the-scenes / process | Process videos stop scroll better when the final output is previewed first. |
| H215 | Behind-the-scenes / process | BTS videos build trust when they reveal a decision, not just aesthetics. |
| H216 | Behind-the-scenes / process | Time-lapse process needs milestone labels to avoid becoming visual noise. |
| H217 | Case study / teardown | Teardowns convert to follows better when they diagnose a specific mechanism rather than praise the example. |
| H218 | Case study / teardown | Case studies save better when they end with a repeatable rule. |
| H219 | Case study / teardown | Showing the failed version first increases attention for teardowns. |
| H220 | Comparison / review | Comparison videos save better when the criteria are named before the options. |
| H221 | Comparison / review | Reviews retain better when the verdict is previewed early and explained later. |
| H222 | Comparison / review | Side-by-side visuals outperform sequential explanations when differences are visual. |
| H223 | Motion graphic / carousel-style video | Carousel-style videos complete better when each slide resolves one micro-question. |
| H224 | Motion graphic / carousel-style video | Motion graphics need restrained animation; too much movement competes with reading. |
| H225 | Motion graphic / carousel-style video | Save rate rises when the final frame summarises the framework as a screenshotable card. |
| H226 | TikTok For You | Full-watch and skip behaviour should be treated as creative diagnostics before hashtags. |
| H227 | TikTok For You | Videos with native conversational openings outperform polished ad-like openings for creator-led advice. |
| H228 | TikTok For You | TikTok FYP videos should show value before asking for context. |
| H229 | TikTok Search | Exact problem phrasing in speech, caption and overlay increases search-match potential. |
| H230 | TikTok Search | Search-first TikToks should answer quickly, then deepen; feed-first TikToks can lead with tension. |
| H231 | TikTok Search | Content-gap topics from Creator Search Insights produce longer-tail views than purely trend-driven topics. |
| H232 | TikTok LIVE | Live clips retain better when edited around a complete question/answer unit. |
| H233 | TikTok LIVE | Live previews convert better when they promise a specific audience outcome. |
| H234 | TikTok LIVE | Gift/comment-heavy live moments need context labels to travel as standalone clips. |
| H235 | TikTok Shop / commerce | Shop videos convert better when the problem is demonstrated before the product link appears. |
| H236 | TikTok Shop / commerce | Creator proof outperforms product spec lists for low-consideration products. |
| H237 | TikTok Shop / commerce | Short objection-handling videos generate more qualified clicks than hype-only demos. |
| H238 | YouTube Shorts feed | First frame behaves like a moving thumbnail and should be designed before the script is finalised. |
| H239 | YouTube Shorts feed | Engaged views are a better scaling metric than raw Shorts views after the 2025 view-count change. |
| H240 | YouTube Shorts feed | Shorts with a series promise convert more subscribers than isolated tips. |
| H241 | YouTube Search / evergreen | Searchable Shorts need direct titles and direct first lines more than mystery hooks. |
| H242 | YouTube Search / evergreen | Shorts that point to a related long video increase total session value. |
| H243 | YouTube Search / evergreen | Evergreen tutorials should prioritise clarity over trend audio. |
| H244 | YouTube long-to-short bridge | Long-form highlights should be cut around one complete tension/payoff loop, not arbitrary timestamps. |
| H245 | YouTube long-to-short bridge | Shorts that preview a larger unresolved question can drive long-form traffic if the payoff remains honest. |
| H246 | YouTube long-to-short bridge | Repurposed long-form clips need new first frames; the long-form opening usually starts too slowly for Shorts. |
| H247 | YouTube comments / reply Shorts | Replying to comments with Shorts increases perceived relevance when the question is shown visually. |
| H248 | YouTube comments / reply Shorts | Comment-reply Shorts convert better when the answer expands into a reusable rule. |
| H249 | YouTube comments / reply Shorts | Controversial comments can power videos, but source/proof must appear early to protect trust. |
| H250 | Instagram Reels feed | Reels should bias toward sendability when the content has identity/social relevance. |
| H251 | Instagram Reels feed | Reels with clean visual hierarchy outperform cluttered meme-style overlays for educational content. |
| H252 | Instagram Reels feed | A strong cover can improve profile-grid conversion even if feed autoplay carries initial reach. |
| H253 | Instagram Trial Reels | Trial Reels are best for cold-audience hook testing, not follower-community content. |
| H254 | Instagram Trial Reels | Formats that win Trial Reels should be scaled only if they also fit the account promise. |
| H255 | Instagram Trial Reels | Trial Reels can reveal whether a niche idea travels beyond existing audience familiarity. |
| H256 | Instagram Stories | Stories work better for relationship and conversion than cold discovery. |
| H257 | Instagram Stories | Polls before links increase link intent by creating participation momentum. |
| H258 | Instagram Stories | Story sequences should use one idea per frame to avoid drop-off. |
| H259 | Instagram profile / pinned posts | Pinned Reels should explain the account promise, not just show the biggest viral hit. |
| H260 | Instagram profile / pinned posts | Covers with explicit series labels increase bingeing from profile visits. |
| H261 | Instagram profile / pinned posts | A clear grid architecture improves follow conversion after a single viral Reel. |
| H262 | LinkedIn native video | LinkedIn videos perform better when the hook names a professional tension, not a generic entertainment premise. |
| H263 | LinkedIn native video | Subtitles and document-style recap frames increase saves for professional education. |
| H264 | LinkedIn native video | Credibility markers matter more on LinkedIn than trend aesthetics. |
| H265 | X / Twitter video | Videos on X need standalone text copy because many starts come from the post text rather than the first frame alone. |
| H266 | X / Twitter video | Contrarian clips drive comments/reposts but need receipts to avoid low-trust dunking. |
| H267 | X / Twitter video | Short clips with one quote-worthy line spread better than dense tutorials. |
| H268 | Pinterest video / idea pins | Pinterest-style videos should prioritise search intent and evergreen utility over fast trend hooks. |
| H269 | Pinterest video / idea pins | Final checklist cards increase saves because Pinterest users often collect future-use content. |
| H270 | Pinterest video / idea pins | Vertical process visuals outperform talking-head intros for DIY/recipe/design content. |
| H271 | Twitch / stream ecosystem | Stream clips travel better when edited into a narrative unit: setup, tension, payoff. |
| H272 | Twitch / stream ecosystem | Creator reaction is the share trigger when the event itself is common. |
| H273 | Twitch / stream ecosystem | Clips that invite viewers back to a scheduled stream need a clear next-live promise. |
| H274 | Classification | If platform and viewer cannot classify the topic in 2 seconds, all downstream signals weaken. |
| H275 | Classification | Repeating one exact problem phrase across speech, overlay and caption improves topic coherence. |
| H276 | Classification | Mixed-topic videos underperform single-promise videos when the audience is cold. |
| H277 | Viewed vs swiped | First-frame clarity has a larger effect on viewed-vs-swiped than final CTA choice. |
| H278 | Viewed vs swiped | Motion in the first half-second improves stop-scroll only when the subject is clear. |
| H279 | Viewed vs swiped | A visible result beats a promise-only first frame for practical content. |
| H280 | 1-second hold | A one-subject frame improves 1-second hold more than added text effects. |
| H281 | 1-second hold | Large faces help 1-second hold for unknown creators, but proof must follow quickly. |
| H282 | 1-second hold | Ambiguous opens can still work if the motion creates immediate curiosity without confusion. |
| H283 | 3-second retention | Problem-first hooks outperform topic-first hooks for cold educational content. |
| H284 | 3-second retention | Direct viewer callouts improve 3-second retention when the target audience is specific. |
| H285 | 3-second retention | Hooks longer than one breath increase early drop-off unless paired with a strong visual payoff. |
| H286 | Average watch time | Average watch rises when each visual reset carries new information. |
| H287 | Average watch time | Mid-video retention depends on micro-payoffs every 5–8 seconds for tutorial content. |
| H288 | Average watch time | Voiceover pacing should slow at inspection moments and speed through setup. |
| H289 | Completion | Completion improves when the ending is foreshadowed but not fully resolved in the hook. |
| H290 | Completion | Navigation text increases completion for videos longer than 45 seconds. |
| H291 | Completion | Shorter videos only improve completion if they preserve the first payoff and final resolution. |
| H292 | Rewatch | Rewatch increases when the video contains a fast side-by-side comparison viewers want to inspect. |
| H293 | Rewatch | Looped endings increase rewatch but can reduce satisfaction if the loop hides the payoff. |
| H294 | Rewatch | Dense templates increase rewatch if the final frame summarises the formula. |
| H295 | Saves | Saves increase when the video offers a reusable asset rather than a one-time insight. |
| H296 | Saves | Final screenshotable cards raise saves for frameworks and recipes. |
| H297 | Saves | Save CTAs work only when the reason to save is explicit. |
| H298 | Sends / shares | Send CTAs beat generic share CTAs when they name a specific recipient. |
| H299 | Sends / shares | Identity-recognition videos send better than purely informational videos in community niches. |
| H300 | Sends / shares | A warning frame increases sends when the recipient can make the same mistake. |
| H301 | Comments | Binary prompts produce more comments than open-ended prompts. |
| H302 | Comments | Comment quality improves when the prompt asks for a useful variable, such as niche, hook, or example. |
| H303 | Comments | Controversial hooks increase comments but should be judged against completion and follow rate. |
| H304 | Follows / subscriptions | Series labels increase follows by making future value predictable. |
| H305 | Follows / subscriptions | Proof-backed content converts more follows than unsupported tips in expert niches. |
| H306 | Follows / subscriptions | High saves with low follows indicate the video is useful but the account promise is unclear. |
| H307 | Profile clicks | Profile clicks rise when the video creates authority curiosity rather than just giving a complete one-off answer. |
| H308 | Profile clicks | Clear account positioning in pinned/profile content converts viral visitors better. |
| H309 | Profile clicks | Strong creator POV increases profile clicks more than neutral utility. |
| H310 | Search long-tail | Search traffic grows when the exact query appears in title/caption and first spoken line. |
| H311 | Search long-tail | Search videos decay slower than trend videos if they solve a persistent problem. |
| H312 | Search long-tail | Hybrid search/feed opens are strongest when they show a result visually and name the query textually. |
| H313 | Trust / negative feedback | Clickbait hooks increase starts but reduce completion and follow conversion when the payoff is weaker than the promise. |
| H314 | Trust / negative feedback | Source/proof overlays reduce negative comments in controversial educational content. |
| H315 | Trust / negative feedback | AI-looking generic templates reduce trust unless paired with original proof, voice or judgment. |
| H316 | First frame | The best first frame shows either result, problem, proof or identity; generic context loses. |
| H317 | First frame | First frames with labelled contrast outperform single-state frames for before/after content. |
| H318 | First frame | A first frame should be designed after choosing the primary action: save, send, comment, follow or search. |
| H319 | Hook | Hooks should name a concrete gap, not vague suspense. |
| H320 | Hook | Contrarian hooks require proof within 5–10 seconds to preserve trust. |
| H321 | Hook | Result-first hooks work best when the result is visually shown, not just claimed. |
| H322 | Captions | Edited captions beat auto captions when speech density is high. |
| H323 | Captions | Captions should summarise speech, not transcribe every filler word. |
| H324 | Captions | Caption placement should follow visual safe zones rather than fixed lower-centre placement. |
| H325 | Text overlays | Overlay text works when it states the frame's job: problem, step, proof, result or CTA. |
| H326 | Text overlays | Huge central overlay text performs worse over dynamic footage than over static or low-motion footage. |
| H327 | Text overlays | Text density should decrease as visual detail increases. |
| H328 | Face | Unknown creators benefit from early face presence if followed by proof quickly. |
| H329 | Face | Facecam improves software tutorials when it adds trust but does not cover the UI target. |
| H330 | Face | Emotional face reactions improve shares when the emotional reaction is the content's social value. |
| H331 | Audio | Voice clarity matters more than trend audio for educational videos. |
| H332 | Audio | Sound effects should punctuate semantic beats, not every cut. |
| H333 | Audio | Silence/low audio can increase seriousness for proof-led content if captions are strong. |
| H334 | Pacing | Cuts should happen when information changes, not when the editor gets bored. |
| H335 | Pacing | Educational videos need slower pacing during proof inspection and faster pacing during setup. |
| H336 | Pacing | The first 10 seconds should contain at least two meaning changes for cold-audience educational content. |
| H337 | Story | Problem-test-result beats pure advice when the advice is not obvious. |
| H338 | Story | Before-after-why is the strongest structure when transformation is visible. |
| H339 | Story | Myth-evidence-correction increases comments but needs a replacement framework to earn saves. |
| H340 | Proof | Proof shown early improves trust even if it slightly lowers casual reach. |
| H341 | Proof | Real examples outperform abstract diagrams for beginner audiences. |
| H342 | Proof | Framework diagrams outperform examples for expert audiences if the example is already obvious. |
| H343 | CTA | CTA should match the content's natural action: save for utility, send for social relevance, comment for choice, follow for series. |
| H344 | CTA | Early soft CTAs can guide attention without feeling like engagement bait. |
| H345 | CTA | A CTA loses power when it asks for two actions at once. |
| H346 | Length | Length should expand only when each extra segment adds proof, tension or payoff. |
| H347 | Length | Very short videos win completion but may lose saves if they omit enough reusable detail. |
| H348 | Length | Longer short-form videos need visible progress markers to prevent perceived drift. |
| H349 | Cover / title | Covers matter most after feed exposure, when viewers visit the profile or search surface. |
| H350 | Cover / title | Titles should be clearer than hooks for search surfaces and more tensioned for feed surfaces. |
| H351 | Cover / title | Positive or useful wording in titles may preserve trust better than exaggerated negative wording. |
| H352 | Comment system | Comment prompts should collect usable research variables, not empty engagement. |
| H353 | Comment system | Top comments can become proof or objections for the next video. |
| H354 | Comment system | Pinned comments can steer interpretation and reduce repeated confusion. |
| H355 | Originality / AI workflow | AI-generated content needs a visible original judgment layer to avoid template fatigue. |
| H356 | Originality / AI workflow | Original tests beat recycled claims for trust-building niches. |
| H357 | Originality / AI workflow | Creator-specific constraints and examples make AI-assisted scripts feel less generic. |
| H358 | SaaS / software | SaaS videos convert better when they show the workflow pain before the feature. |
| H359 | SaaS / software | Screen recordings need mobile-safe zooms and annotations to avoid UI blindness. |
| H360 | SaaS / software | Comparison against an old workflow makes value clearer than listing features. |
| H361 | Education / study | Study content saves better when delivered as a repeatable method, not motivation. |
| H362 | Education / study | Worked examples outperform abstract advice for technical learning. |
| H363 | Education / study | Spaced/review prompts increase follows if the account promises a continuing learning sequence. |
| H364 | Fitness | Wrong/right demonstrations outperform correct-only form tutorials. |
| H365 | Fitness | Follow-along workouts complete better when timers are visible. |
| H366 | Fitness | Fitness transformations need process proof to avoid feeling unrealistic. |
| H367 | Food / cooking | Food videos stop scroll with final dish or texture, not ingredient setup. |
| H368 | Food / cooking | Recipe videos save better when measurements are on screen long enough to screenshot. |
| H369 | Food / cooking | One-pan/low-effort framing increases sends because the recipient category is obvious. |
| H370 | Beauty / fashion | Transformation-first visuals outperform product-first intros. |
| H371 | Beauty / fashion | Use-case framing such as workplace/date/night-out increases saves over generic styling. |
| H372 | Beauty / fashion | Side-by-side shade/fit labels reduce rewatch frustration and increase saves. |
| H373 | Finance / money | Finance content needs credibility cues earlier than entertainment content. |
| H374 | Finance / money | Simple calculators or examples save better than abstract money principles. |
| H375 | Finance / money | Fear-based hooks may raise starts but reduce trust unless paired with measured, sourced advice. |
| H376 | Real estate / property | Property videos stop scroll with the strongest room/feature, not the front door. |
| H377 | Real estate / property | Neighbourhood/context clips generate more saves than pure listing tours for buyers researching later. |
| H378 | Real estate / property | Before/after renovation clips share better when cost/time constraints are stated. |
| H379 | Travel | Travel videos save better when framed as itinerary/useful route, not only scenery. |
| H380 | Travel | Hidden-gem framing increases shares but needs proof of accessibility/cost to earn saves. |
| H381 | Travel | Travel first frames should show the most distinctive place-specific visual immediately. |
| H382 | Music / performance | Music clips retain better when the best moment arrives in the first 3 seconds. |
| H383 | Music / performance | Process/behind-the-song clips convert follows better than finished performance alone. |
| H384 | Music / performance | Lyric/caption overlays increase shareability when the line is identity-relevant. |
| H385 | Art / design | Art process videos stop scroll with either final work or a dramatic transformation moment. |
| H386 | Art / design | Design critique videos follow better when they explain the decision logic. |
| H387 | Art / design | Before/after design videos save better when the design rule is extracted at the end. |
| H388 | Gaming | Skill tutorials save better when they freeze the exact input/mechanic. |
| H389 | Gaming | High-stakes clips need the stakes label before the play begins. |
| H390 | Gaming | Patch/meta commentary gets search long-tail when the exact game/version is in title and first line. |
| H391 | Ecommerce / retail | Product videos convert better from problem demonstration than aesthetic product display. |
| H392 | Ecommerce / retail | Objection-handling clips improve qualified clicks for higher-consideration items. |
| H393 | Ecommerce / retail | Comparison grids increase saves when buyers need to decide later. |
| H394 | B2B / professional services | B2B videos should lead with business pain, not creator personality. |
| H395 | B2B / professional services | Case studies convert better when the constraint, intervention and outcome are all visible. |
| H396 | B2B / professional services | Authority content needs original frameworks or data, not generic tips. |
| H397 | Career / job / study life | Career videos save better when they include scripts, templates or checklists. |
| H398 | Career / job / study life | Day-in-the-life videos retain better when framed by a concrete lesson or tension. |
| H399 | Career / job / study life | Interview/resume tips share better when aimed at a specific recipient category. |
| H400 | Local services | Local service videos convert better when they show the messy before state first. |
| H401 | Local services | Geographic labels improve qualified discovery but may narrow total reach. |
| H402 | Local services | Customer question videos build trust more efficiently than promo videos. |
| H403 | Health / wellbeing - non-medical | Wellbeing content should avoid overclaiming and use practical, low-risk routines to preserve trust. |
| H404 | Health / wellbeing - non-medical | Routine videos save better when they fit a clear time/context such as morning, lunch break or pre-sleep. |
| H405 | Health / wellbeing - non-medical | Relief framing works better than fear framing for sustainable wellbeing content. |
| H406 | Variant design | One major variable per test produces cleaner learning than changing hook, text, length and CTA simultaneously. |
| H407 | Variant design | A 3-variant test is usually enough to detect direction before producing a 10-variant batch. |
| H408 | Variant design | Reusing the same body with different first frames is the fastest way to test scroll-stop. |
| H409 | Time windows | 24h metrics reveal packaging and early audience fit; 7d/30d metrics reveal search and durable value. |
| H410 | Time windows | A video that loses 24h but wins 30d should be classified as evergreen, not failed. |
| H411 | Time windows | A video that wins 24h but dies by 7d should be treated as trend/emotion-led unless it also gains search traffic. |
| H412 | Cohort comparison | Compare videos within the same topic cluster, not across unrelated topics. |
| H413 | Cohort comparison | Follower-heavy and non-follower-heavy posts need separate diagnosis. |
| H414 | Cohort comparison | Small accounts should judge follow conversion and saves more heavily than vanity view totals. |
| H415 | Creative genome | Tagging first-frame type reveals repeatable patterns that raw view counts hide. |
| H416 | Creative genome | Winner mechanisms are more useful than winning topics. |
| H417 | Creative genome | Failed videos with strong saves can become stronger after packaging revision. |
| H418 | Pre-post scoring | Videos scoring below threshold on first-frame clarity should be rebuilt before posting. |
| H419 | Pre-post scoring | A video should declare its primary action before scripting. |
| H420 | Pre-post scoring | Scripts with no first payoff under 7 seconds should be considered high-risk for cold audiences. |
| H421 | Postmortems | Every postmortem should identify the failed gate, not just label the video good or bad. |
| H422 | Postmortems | Comments should be coded as confusion, disagreement, identity, request or praise. |
| H423 | Postmortems | Videos with high completion and low action need stronger save/send/follow design, not better hooks. |
| H424 | Repurposing | Repurposed long clips need new native openings for each platform. |
| H425 | Repurposing | The same insight can produce separate save, send and comment variants. |
| H426 | Repurposing | Repurposing should preserve the mechanism, not the exact edit. |
| H427 | Series testing | A series should test one stable promise with variable examples. |
| H428 | Series testing | Series intros should be shorter as audience familiarity grows. |
| H429 | Series testing | A named series increases follow conversion only if the name communicates future value. |
| H430 | Risk control | Controversy should be tested against trust metrics, not just comments. |
| H431 | Risk control | Ragebait can be killed even if it wins comments when it loses completion or follow conversion. |
| H432 | Risk control | AI/template content should be audited for sameness before scaling volume. |
| H433 | Decision rules | Scale when a mechanism wins at least two of: retention, save/send, follow, or 7d durability. |
| H434 | Decision rules | Kill when a tactic improves starts but consistently damages completion and trust. |
| H435 | Decision rules | Iterate when the content value is strong but the packaging gate failed. |
